title: chenopodium ambrosioides var. incisum
intro: Chenopodium ambrosioides var. incisum, also known as Mexican tea or epazote, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Amaranthaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and carminative effects. The health benefits of this plant include relief from digestive issues, such as bloating, gas, and nausea, as well as its use in treating menstrual cramps and respiratory problems. Chenopodium ambrosioides var. incisum can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional folk medicine in Mexico and other parts of Latin America.
